mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand across consumer and capital goods sectors, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during periods of supply chain disruption or when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the 230 threshold, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL stays above 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and total nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized economic expansion, which boosts equity risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark with a Sharpe ratio greater than 0.5 over any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll gains consistently fail to correlate with subsequent quarterly equity returns with a Spearman rho below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with elevated inflation signals stagflationary pressures that squeeze corporate profitability and trigger risk-off selling, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate exceeding 55% in out-of-sample validation.
